title: On-call triage
problem: Responders lose time collecting context from fragmented incident tooling.
solution: Ravah correlates alerts, logs, and deploy history into one evidence-first timeline.
output: Prioritized probable causes with suggested runbook actions.
impact: Faster handoffs and lower escalation fatigue for on-call engineers.

title: CI/CD failures
problem: Pipeline failures are noisy and root cause takes too long to isolate.
solution: Ravah highlights risky commits, flaky tests, and environment drift before release.
output: Failure clustering and rollback-ready remediation suggestions.
impact: Higher deployment confidence and fewer production regressions.

title: Noisy alerts
problem: Teams receive duplicate alerts that hide real service degradation.
solution: Ravah deduplicates alerts and surfaces impact-ranked incidents.
output: Actionable incident queue with service ownership context.
impact: Reduced alert fatigue and improved response focus.

title: Postmortems
problem: Post-incident analysis is manual and inconsistent across teams.
solution: Ravah auto-builds timelines and captures remediation tasks from evidence.
output: Structured postmortem draft with owners and follow-up actions.
impact: More reliable learning loops and better prevention planning.
